title: urfave/cli: fix regression in short-opts parsing
body:
Add the actual argument, not the one we're looking for when searching
the to-be-translated short-opt string.  Otherwise, we're likely to hit
an infinite loop.

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <vrothberg@suse.com>

Cc: @giuseppe
Fixes: https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/pull/1046#issuecomment-403547417

comments.body:
thanks for such a quick fix!  It solves the issue I was seeing but I am still having problems to run the container:

```
$ sudo bin/podman run --uidmap 0:0:10000 --gidmap 0:0:10000  -ti --rm alpine echo hello
cannot mount /var/run/netns/cni-d4f7d5a9-6d10-f0c5-3434-eb1a338a9538: no such file or directory
```

The same command works if I revert 49fe03c626dc10781f2d3241a489c95515af9db4 on top of `origin/master`

> The same command works if I revert 49fe03c on top of origin/master

It doesn't work for me if I revert. I revendored `urfave/cli` to be sure and that yields the exact same error (without using `-it` but `-i -t`). I conclude that some commit in-between broke that.
Sorry, that was red herring. The flagSet has to reset before doing another parse. I'll add a test to catch that regression.

@vrothberg thanks for the hint, it works for me if I specify `-i -t`, also it works if I reorder the options and `-ti` is before `--uidmap`
```
$ sudo bin/podman run --uidmap 0:0:10000 --gidmap 0:0:10000  -t -i --rm alpine echo hello
hello
$ sudo bin/podman run --uidmap 0:0:10000 --gidmap 0:0:10000  -ti --rm alpine echo hello
cannot mount /var/run/netns/cni-1734e508-90ca-c5f1-3f2c-6ebf4a30cc96: no such file or directory
$ sudo bin/podman run -ti  --uidmap 0:0:10000 --gidmap 0:0:10000  --rm alpine echo hello
hello

```

I've applied the patch from this PR on top of c7424b69911222c2dc92a41308685f1e6d36fb53
@giuseppe Thanks a lot for checking. I have updated the PR now including the fix for urfave/cli (revendored from vrothberg/cli) with a e2e test ontop to avoid a potential future regression.

thanks a lot, it solves the issue I was seeing.

LGTM

/cc @mheon @baude @rhatdan
